description_long: This object is a vintage computer keyboard integrated into a compact, rectangular black plastic chassis. The keyboard features a full set of gray keys, including a standard alphanumeric section and a dedicated blue directional pad on the right side, which is uncommon in modern keyboards. Above the keys, there are several function buttons and indicator lights, as well as a cartridge slot and various ports on the rear panel, suggesting it is part of a home computer system rather than a standalone keyboard. The surface is mostly matte with some glossy highlights, and the keys are slightly concave for ergonomic typing. The right side of the device displays a prominent blue square with directional arrows, and there are visible brand markings and labels on the front left. The rear panel includes multiple input/output ports, such as cartridge slots, audio jacks, and possibly a power connector, indicating its use as a retro computing device.
description: A vintage black computer keyboard with gray keys, a blue directional pad, and multiple rear ports, integrated into a compact chassis. It features brand markings and a distinctive retro design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard layout, blue directional pad, and brand logo.
description_view_1: Left angled view highlighting the keyboard's depth, side profile, and the raised section at the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying various ports, cartridge slot, and connectors along the back panel.
description_view_3: Right angled view emphasizing the blue directional pad and the keyboard's right-side profile.
attribute_key_layout: Full-size with directional pad (retro home computer layout)
